channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Nvidia revenue forecasts for first quarter exceed estimates
item.description: Nvidia forecast first-quarter revenue above market estimates on Wednesday, expecting robust demand for its leading AI chips to persist as businesses spend heavily to expand generative artificial intelligence infrastructure.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Amazon’s AI-powered Alexa+ wants to be your best friend—for $19.99 per month
item.description: Amazon on Wednesday unveiled a generative-AI infused Alexa that it says will allow the popular voice assistant to have more personality, check a user’s tone and even plan romantic dates.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Former Navy comptroller shares how financial management can successfully impact decision-making
item.description: Modern financial systems offer the ideal way to tie budget to strategy and improve decision-making, explains Russ Rumbaugh, who left the Navy in January 2025.
item.dc:creator: Vanessa Roberts, Federal News Network

item.title: EPA head urges Trump to reconsider scientific finding that underpins climate action, AP sources say
item.description: The head of the Environmental Protection Agency has privately urged the Trump administration to reconsider a scientific finding that's long been the central basis for U.S. action against climate change.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Daly, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Malware exposes 3.9 billion passwords in huge cybersecurity threat
item.description: Kurt the CyberGuy offers tips on how you can protect your devices against infostealer malware, which was used to infect 4.3 million machines last year.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Medical tech heart health company expands with South Bay office deal
item.description: A medical tech company helping to combat heart problems has leased a Santa Clara site in an expansion.
item.dc:creator: George Avalos, The Mercury News

item.title: 3 California residents arrested in reported ‘pig butchering’ investment schemes totaling $13 million
item.description: Three people were arrested after reportedly creating shell companies that stole millions from victims of investment scams.
item.dc:creator: Andrea Klick, The Mercury News

item.title: FCC chairman climbs WKRG broadcast tower to highlight, promote industry
item.description: The nation's top communications regulator made a stop in Baldwin County Monday morning and you might be surprised why.
item.dc:creator: Debbie Williams, The Hill

item.title: Musk business entanglements loom over CFPB siege
item.description: Elon Musk’s plans to add payment services to X, his social media platform, are looming large over the Trump administration’s push to dismantle the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau (CFPB), The tech billionaire has long voiced his hopes of turning X into an “everything app” that would also functio
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: A must-try Android email upgrade
item.description: Ahem: I have two separate confessions to make, both equally embarrassing. Confession #1 is that I love email. I’m totally not kidding, either.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: How LinkedIn became luxury fashion’s newest runway
item.description: As Fashion Week takes over New York, London, and Milan, designers aren’t just showcasing their collections on the runway—they’re taking over LinkedIn.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: Former VA cybersecurity official warns DOGE access threatens sensitive data
item.description: A former Department of Veterans Affairs cybersecurity official warned that the Department of Government Efficiency’s (DOGE) access to the department’s information threatens sensitive data stored within the organization.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: New Relic boosts observability platform with AI intelligence
item.description: New Relic announced updates to its Intelligent Observability Platform this week, which the company says will enable a unified view of system relationships and dependencies and intelligently connect technical systems with business context.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Google rolls out free version of Gemini Code Assist for individuals
item.description: Google is rolling out a free version of Gemini Code Assist, its tool for code completion, code generation, and code chat, with the aim of helping individual developers improve their productivity.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Review: Gemini Code Assist is good at coding
item.description: Given that Microsoft/GitHub/Azure and Amazon/AWS offer AI coding assistants, namely GitHub Copilot and Amazon Q Developer, which work as add-ins to Visual Studio Code and other IDEs, it was inevitable that Google/GCP would introduce one of its own.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: What the private sector can teach government about cloud computing
item.description: Cloud computing has been at the forefront of government IT strategies for many years. When cloud computing first became a thing in 2008, the government led the way, including NIST creating a definition of cloud computing. I worked closely with them then and watched the whole thing play out.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Apple shareholders reject proposal to scrap company's diversity programs
item.description: Apple shareholders rebuffed an attempt to pressure the technology trendsetter into joining President Donald Trump’s push to scrub corporate programs designed to diversify its workforce.
item.dc:creator: Michael Liedtke, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Apple announces $500 billion investment in US amid tariff threats that could affect the iPhone
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Apple announced Monday that it plans to invest more than $500 billion in the United States over the next four years, including plans to hire 20,000 people and build a new server factory in Texas.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Elon Musk's underground digital detective reveals all to NewsNation
item.description: NewsNation's Brian Entin has the first interview with the tech genius using AI to discover alleged government waste and corruption.
item.dc:creator: Brian Entin, NewsNation

item.title: Intel targets edge, high-performance computing with extended Xeon 6 chip line
item.description: Intel has expanded its Xeon 6 line of processors, adding models 6700/6500 for high-performance cores and edge computing devices to the family. The new Xeon 6 processors feature Performance-cores (P-cores) for high performance computing.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Brazil Supreme Court justice orders Rumble suspension nationwide for alleged non-compliance
item.description: SAO PAULO (AP) — A Brazilian Supreme Court justice on Friday ordered the suspension of video-sharing platform Rumble in the South American nation for allegedly refusing to comply with court orders.
item.dc:creator: MAURICIO SAVARESE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Is your phone listening to everything you say? It’s complicated
item.description: Google, Instagram, Facebook and weather apps can track your location and use that data to serve you ads; here's what you can do to protect your privacy.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How artificial intelligence is energizing information technology
item.description: "Data is getting even more gravity in this space because the more data you have, the more accuracy these parameters get in your model," said Bill Vass. The post How artificial intelligence is energizing information technology first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: GenAI is my copilot? Developers weigh pains and gains
item.description: Developers are quicker than most to embrace generative AI tools as part of their workflow. That can mean enhanced productivity and easing cognitive burdens, but it also means learning new technologies while they’re still being built.
item.dc:creator: Info World
